# pheno-port-adapter

> Hexagonal port/adapter primitives for the pheno-* fleet. A
> `PortAdapter` trait that every transport-agnostic consumer can
> depend on, plus concrete TCP and Unix-domain-socket transport
> adapters. Sits at the substrate layer — consumers write against
> the trait, not against a specific transport.

## Public API

```rust
use pheno_port_adapter::{AdapterError, Connection, PortAdapter};
use pheno_port_adapter::adapters::{TcpAdapter, UnixAdapter};

#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]
pub enum AdapterError {
    #[error("connect failed: {0}")]
    ConnectFailed(String),
    #[error("disconnect failed: {0}")]
    DisconnectFailed(String),
    #[error("health check failed: {0}")]
    HealthCheckFailed(String),
    #[error("timeout")]
    Timeout,
}

#[derive(Debug)]
pub struct Connection { pub(crate) id: String }

pub trait PortAdapter: Send + Sync {
    fn name(&self) -> &str;
    fn health(&self) -> Result<(), AdapterError>;
    fn connect(&self, endpoint: &str) -> Result<Connection, AdapterError>;
    fn disconnect(&self) -> Result<(), AdapterError>;
}

pub mod adapters; // TcpAdapter, UnixAdapter
```

## Quickstart

```toml
# Cargo.toml
[dependencies]
pheno-port-adapter = "0.1"
```

```rust
use pheno_port_adapter::adapters::TcpAdapter;
use pheno_port_adapter::PortAdapter;
let adapter = TcpAdapter;
assert_eq!(adapter.name(), "tcp");
let conn = adapter.connect("tcp://localhost:8080")?;
adapter.disconnect()?;
```

## Conventions

- Conventional Commits (`feat:`, `fix:`, `chore:`, `docs:`, `refactor:`).
- rustfmt + clippy (`-D warnings`); `#![warn(missing_docs)]` on the
  public API.
- 80% lib coverage gate (ADR-040); see `llvm-cov.toml` and the
  `coverage` job in `.github/workflows/ci.yml`.
- Hexagonal pattern: `PortAdapter` trait + concrete `Adapter` impl
  (ADR-014). Consumers depend on the trait, not on a transport.
- `thiserror` for error types; `AdapterError` is the single error
  surface (no per-transport variants).
- `Connection` is an opaque handle; transport-specific connection
  state stays inside each `Adapter` impl.
- 5 inline unit tests in `src/lib.rs` cover the trait contract;
  add a `tests/` integration suite when you add a new transport.

## See also

- `src/lib.rs` — trait, error type, and inline `MockAdapter` tests.
- `src/adapters/tcp.rs`, `src/adapters/unix.rs` — concrete
  transports.
- `AGENTS.md` — agent context.
- `WORKLOG.md` — change history (v2.1 schema).
- `CHANGELOG.md` — release notes.
- `LICENSE-MIT` — MIT license.
- Sibling: `pheno-config` (typed config), `pheno-context` (request
  context), `pheno-flags` (feature flags).
